title: Boat Rental in Raja Ampat: Prices, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Raja Ampat in West Papua offers some of the world’s best diving and cruising, with charters typically departing from Sorong harbour. A half-day speedboat costs from €150 to €250 and a crewed liveaboard from €600 per day. Use BoatHire24 to compare verified listings across this archipelago.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Raja Ampat?

Prices range from €150 for a shared tour to over €1,000 per day for a crewed yacht. Below is a typical price breakdown:

  • Shared tour: from €80 per person for a full-day snorkelling trip to Pianemo.
  • Small motorboat, half-day: from €150 for a 6-metre local boat, licensed for up to six guests.
  • Catamaran, full-day: from €500 for a Lagoon 380 with skipper, sleeping up to ten.
  • Crewed yacht, all-inclusive: from €1,000 per night for a luxury catamaran like a Sunseeker 50.

For an accurate view of current inventory and deals, you can hire a boat directly through BoatHire24 and filter by budget and boat type.

Which boat to rent in Raja Ampat?

The best choice depends on your group size and itinerary, from speedboats for day trips to yachts for multi-day exploration. Consider these options:

  1. Speedboat (e.g. Olympic 490), ideal for 4-6 guests on day trips to the Wayag viewpoint or Kabui Bay; from €230 per day. Check this speedboat listing on BoatHire24 for availability.
  2. Local fishing boat (jukung), a budget option for 2-4 guests, perfect for snorkelling in shallow reefs; from €120 for a half-day.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 40), stable and spacious, sleeps up to eight, suited for week-long cruises through the Dampier Strait; from €800 per day.
  4. Crewed yacht (e.g. Azimut 50), luxury with a captain and cook, for 6-8 guests exploring Misool Island; from €1,200 per day.

Where to set off in Raja Ampat

Most trips start from Sorong harbour on the mainland, with additional pickups possible at Waisai and several island piers. Key departure points include:

  • Sorong harbour, the main gateway, with daily ferries to Waisai and motorboat charters to most islands.
  • Waisai marina, capital of the archipelago, offering fuel and a government jetty.
  • Arborek jetty, a village pier in the Daerah Perlindungan Laut (MPA), used for diving trips.
  • Kri Island beach landings, only accessible by small boats, popular for resort transfers.
  • Pianemo viewpoint landing, a basic dock near the famous karst islands.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Raja Ampat?

Yes, a valid ICC or equivalent is required for bareboat charters, but most tourists hire a skipper who holds local endorsements. BoatHire24 listings always state whether a licence is needed; the operator will confirm exact requirements before booking.

Local tip: book a crewed vessel for the Raja Ampat Marine Park, the skipper knows the permitted anchorages and reef-friendly moorings.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Raja Ampat?

The peak season runs from October to April, with calm seas and the east monsoon wind. May to September sees stronger westerly winds and rougher passages between Misool and Salawati. In 2026, plan your trip between November and March for the best weather and visibility.

At a glance

  • Price band: €150, €1,200 per day
  • Peak months: November to March
  • Trip length: half-day to 14 days
  • Typical capacity: 4, 10 guests
  • Currency: EUR (but USD often accepted locally)

Questions about boat rental in Raja Ampat

Can I rent a boat for a single day from Sorong?

Yes, many operators offer day trips from Sorong harbour to nearby islands like Birie and Batanta, returning in the late afternoon.

How far is it from Sorong to Wayag?

The distance is about 50 nautical miles, taking two to three hours by speedboat depending on sea conditions.

Are meals included on a crewed charter?

Most crewed yachts include full board; half-day speedboat rentals usually provide snacks and water.

Do I need to pay park fees separately?

Yes, all visitors must purchase a Raja Ampat Marine Park entry tag (currently covered by some charter packages).

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Raja Ampat and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
